legacy.full_text: I’m convinced that all these guys who come up with proposals to “fix the fertility rate” have never talked to a woman. They treat it like an economics problem they can optimize, or even worse they just tell women to have kids to “save civilization.” If you actually spend time

result.text:
I’m convinced that all these guys who come up with proposals to “fix the fertility rate” have never talked to a woman. They treat it like an economics problem they can optimize, or even worse they just tell women to have kids to “save civilization.” If you actually spend time with women who aren’t having kids, you’ll find they almost all fall into one of five buckets:

1. Dating problems. They were in some 5+ year relationship (or a series of relationships) that stole their youth and left them so jaded that they’ve given up on finding a man who could be a good father.

2. Family problems. They come from such a dysfunctional home that their own childhood holds few happy memories, and they’re terrified of recreating those conditions with their own children.

3. Health problems. A ton more women than you think have conditions like endometriosis or PCOS or other health complications that can affect fertility and make having kids more dangerous or harder to do.

4. Career goals. They’re convinced that they have something huge and unique to contribute to the world either professionally or creatively, whereas “every woman can have a kid,” and so to them, having children sounds like a waste of their potential, like giving up.

5. Lifestyle goals. They’re really into traveling and being independent and getting into “adventures,” they want to explore the world and experience everything, and the idea of giving all that up to sit at home and raise kids makes them want to die.

If you really want to fix the fertility rate, and you’re not addressing at least a few of these, your solution is useless.

Nobody hated Mbappe before he joined Madri really? Lmao 🤣

result.text:
Nobody hated Mbappe before he joined Madri really? Lmao 🤣 

When he renewed with Psg in 2022, the backlash from Madrid fans was massive. It got even worse after that interview where he was perceived to have disrespected the club many fans openly said Madrid should never sign him again.

Then there was the constant back-and-forth with Florentino Pérez, which made things even more frustrating. It felt like he was playing with the club for years using their interest as leverage while never making a clear decision.

The repeated transfer saga didn’t help either. Every summer it was the same story, and by 2023, with the contract clause drama at PSG, many fans were already tired of it.

On top of that, the reports about his influence at PSG and the massive contract he signed made some Madrid fans feel like he prioritized money and control over legacy
something that doesn’t align with the club’s values.

It wasn’t just Madrid fans either. A lot of Messi and Neymar Jr. fans grew frustrated with his authoritative attitude at PSG. The constant tension, selfishness, ego and lack of chemistry between them created division within the fanbase, with many feeling he was trying to position himself above player like MESSI and Ney.

Even during the 2022 Champions League run, when Vinícius Júnior scored that iconic goal against Manchester City, commentators were already saying: “Who needs Mbappé? They’ve got Vinicius Jr.”

So no, he was massively hated before he joined us.
